vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2168: feat(dashboard): global navigation affordance to Flywheel openQuestions from anywhere

plan.title: feat(dashboard): global navigation affordance to Flywheel openQuestions from anywhere
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: FlywheelStatus.openQuestions[] (packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts:224) are only visible on the Flywheel status surface. The operator has no way to jump to them from anywhere in the dashboard, so time-sensitive decisions (e.g. releasing MIN merges blocked on the per-project UAT gate) sit unseen until they happen to open the Flywheel page.
narratives.Proposal: Add a persistent indicator to the app bar's right-side status group (App/AppChrome.tsx:190-226), rendered on every route. It shows the open-question count when >0 (hidden at 0), updates live over /ws/rpc, and on click navigates to the Flywheel tab and reveals the existing 'Open Questions for the Next Tick' section (FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx:271-279) with full question text. A small shared signal module (lib/flywheelReveal.ts) decouples the indicator from the Flywheel page. Presentation-only: reuses subscribeFlywheelStatus (lib/wsTransport.ts:283) and GET /api/flywheel/current; no new server data path.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No change to how openQuestions are produced or emitted (server/orchestrator untouched).
- No new dedicated openQuestions drawer or route — reuse the Flywheel status tab.
- No badge in the left Sidebar — the indicator goes in the top app bar.
- No mark-as-read, dismissal, or per-question persistence.
- No change to the existing flywheel-live sidebar nav badge.
- No restyle of the pre-existing (non-conformant) running-agent pill in AppChrome.

autoDecisions.summary: Placement = app bar right-side status group (App/AppChrome.tsx:190-226).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue's suggested placement and is where every other global system indicator already lives; AppChrome renders on every route, satisfying 'from anywhere'.

autoDecisions.summary: Color-signal = --warning (amber), non-pill badge with 8% bg / 32% border tint.
autoDecisions.rationale: pan-style-guide maps 'a human must act / awaiting approval' to amber; an open question is operator-attention. Honors NFR-1 (no rounded-full pill, no bold).

autoDecisions.summary: Reveal target = existing 'Open Questions for the Next Tick' section, not a new drawer.
autoDecisions.rationale: Smallest change that shows each question's full text (AC2); reuses the surface that already renders it.

autoDecisions.summary: Live data reuses the FlywheelPage subscription pattern; no run-lifecycle query in the indicator.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps it minimal and avoids a new data path (NFR-2). /api/flywheel/current returns null when paused, zeroing the count on refetch (hazard H1).

autoDecisions.summary: Decouple via lib/flywheelReveal.ts shared signal module rather than an inline window CustomEvent.
autoDecisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating an event-name string across two components and makes the reveal contract unit-testable.

items.title: Add lib/flywheelReveal.ts reveal-signal contract module

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/flywheelReveal.ts: a tiny module that is the contract between the global indicator and the Flywheel page so neither imports the other. Export `requestRevealOpenQuestions(): void` (sets a module-level sticky-pending boolean to true, then notifies all current listeners), `consumePendingReveal(): boolean` (returns the pending flag and resets it to false), and `subscribeRevealOpenQuestions(cb: () => void): () => void` (adds cb to a module-level Set and returns an unsubscribe that deletes it). No window/global-event usage, no React. This bead ships a new, unused module plus its test — tree stays working.

items.title: Given no prior request, consumePendingReveal() returns false; after requestRevealOpenQuestions(), the next consumePendingReveal() returns true and a subsequent call returns false (one-shot).
Given a callback registered via subscribeRevealOpenQuestions(), when requestRevealOpenQuestions() runs then the callback receives the call synchronously.
Given the unsubscribe returned by subscribeRevealOpenQuestions() has run, when requestRevealOpenQuestions() runs then the removed callback receives nothing.
A new test file src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/__tests__/flywheelReveal.test.ts exercises ac1-ac3 and passes under npm test.
Add OpenQuestionsIndicator component (live count + style-guide badge)

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/OpenQuestionsIndicator.tsx. Props: { onActivate: () => void }. Internally track the latest FlywheelStatus: seed once from GET /api/flywheel/current, then apply live pushes via subscribeFlywheelStatus from lib/wsTransport.ts (mirror FlywheelPage.tsx:373-395 — including a ~5s setInterval refetch of /api/flywheel/current and full cleanup: clear the interval and call the unsubscribe in the effect cleanup, hazard H4). Derive count = status?.openQuestions.length ?? 0. When count === 0, render nothing (null) — AC1 hidden state. When count > 0, render a button styled per pan-style-guide: NOT a pill (use rounded-sm, h-5, font-medium, never font-bold/font-semibold/rounded-full), color-signal --warning with the 8%/32% tint (bg-warning/8 border-warning/32 text-warning-foreground or the nearest existing warning badge class in the repo), a short label/icon plus the count, with title/aria-label like `${count} open question(s) for the Flywheel`. onClick calls requestRevealOpenQuestions() from lib/flywheelReveal.ts then props.onActivate(). Do NOT copy the existing emerald rounded-full running-agent pill (hazard H3).

items.title: Given a FlywheelStatus with openQuestions.length === 0 (or /api/flywheel/current resolving null and no pushes), the component renders nothing (no badge in the DOM).
Given openQuestions.length === 2, the component renders a visible badge showing the count 2.

When a new snapshot arrives via the subscribeFlywheelStatus listener (count 0 -> 3), the rendered count updates to 3 with no remount/reload (FR-3); the test drives this by invoking the captured listener from a mocked subscribeFlywheelStatus.
When the badge is clicked, the component sends requestRevealOpenQuestions() and the onActivate prop exactly once each.
The rendered badge markup contains no `rounded-full`, `font-bold`, or `font-semibold` class and uses a --warning signal token (NFR-1); asserted in the test by inspecting className.
Render the indicator in the app bar and thread navigate-to-Flywheel

narrative.Action: Wire the indicator into the global chrome. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/App/AppChrome.tsx: import OpenQuestionsIndicator, add `onNavigateFlywheel: () => void` to AppChromeProps, and render <OpenQuestionsIndicator onActivate={onNavigateFlywheel} /> inside the right-side status group (the `flex shrink-0 items-center gap-2` div at AppChrome.tsx:190-226, e.g. just before <SystemMenu />). In src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx, pass `onNavigateFlywheel={() => setActiveTab('flywheel')}` to <AppChrome /> (the render block at App.tsx:672-691; setActiveTab is defined at App.tsx:358). Because AppChrome renders on every route, this delivers the 'from anywhere' guarantee (FR-1) and the navigation half of FR-2.

items.title: AppChrome renders <OpenQuestionsIndicator> within its right-side status group; verified by a test that renders AppChrome (mocking child data sources / subscribeFlywheelStatus to supply openQuestions) and finds the indicator badge when count > 0.
When the indicator is activated, AppChrome sends the onNavigateFlywheel prop (App.tsx routes it to setActiveTab('flywheel')); the test asserts onNavigateFlywheel fires on click.
npm run typecheck passes with the new required AppChromeProps.onNavigateFlywheel supplied from App.tsx (no missing-prop or unused-prop errors).
Reveal the openQuestions section on the Flywheel page when activated

narrative.Action: Make the Flywheel page act on the reveal signal. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, give the Section helper (FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx:113) an optional `id?: string` prop applied to its <section>, and pass id="flywheel-open-questions" to the 'Open Questions for the Next Tick' Section (FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx:271). In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx add a useEffect that: (a) on mount, if consumePendingReveal() from lib/flywheelReveal.ts is true, performs reveal; and (b) registers subscribeRevealOpenQuestions(reveal) and returns its unsubscribe (warm case). `reveal` = setActiveTab('status') then requestAnimationFrame(() => document.getElementById('flywheel-open-questions')?.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' })) (hazard H2 — scroll after the status tab renders). Cold navigation already shows the status tab by default (FlywheelPage.tsx:294); this adds the forced-status-tab + scroll for completeness and the warm case.

items.title: The 'Open Questions for the Next Tick' section renders with id="flywheel-open-questions"; asserted in FlywheelStatusDetails.test.tsx.

Given a pending reveal (consumePendingReveal() true at mount), FlywheelPage sets its left tab to 'status' so the openQuestions list is rendered; asserted by a FlywheelPage test that primes the signal then mounts the page.
Given FlywheelPage is mounted on a non-status left tab, when requestRevealOpenQuestions() runs then the page shows the 'status' left tab and renders the openQuestions list.
When FlywheelPage unmounts it runs the unsubscribe returned from the effect, so a later requestRevealOpenQuestions() throws nothing and updates no unmounted state.
